inclusion_criteria: Randomized controlled trials (including follow-up studies of RCT cohorts) comparing a lifestyle intervention to standard care or no lifestyle intervention.

Population: Pregnant women with obesity (e.g., pre-pregnancy or early-pregnancy BMI ≥30 kg/m2 or study-defined obesity) and their offspring; offspring outcomes assessed in infancy (≤2 months) or early childhood (3–7 years). Mixed overweight/obese cohorts are eligible only if data for the obese subgroup are reported or are the majority and analyzable.

Intervention: Preconception or antenatal lifestyle programs targeting diet, physical activity/exercise, or combined behavioral lifestyle interventions (with or without counseling/education components).

Outcomes: At least one offspring cardiovascular outcome reported, including cardiac structure (e.g., interventricular septal thickness, LV mass), cardiac function (systolic/diastolic), or vascular parameters (blood pressure, resting heart rate, arterial stiffness/thickening such as PWV, AIx, cIMT).
exclusion_criteria: Non-randomized designs (e.g., observational, quasi-experimental, single-arm), trials without a standard care/no lifestyle control, or comparisons to active non-lifestyle interventions only.

Interventions that are not lifestyle-based or not delivered preconception/antenatally, including pharmacologic agents (e.g., metformin), surgery (e.g., bariatric), device-based treatments, supplement-only interventions, or postpartum-only programs.

Populations without maternal obesity or where obesity status is not reported; studies limited to normal-weight or overweight without extractable data for participants with obesity; non-human/animal studies.

Studies not reporting eligible offspring cardiovascular outcomes, outcomes measured only after 7 years of age, or studies lacking original data (protocols, reviews, editorials, conference abstracts with insufficient data).
screening_criteria.description: Screening criteria to identify RCTs of preconception or antenatal lifestyle interventions among pregnancies with obesity that report offspring cardiac remodeling or related cardiovascular outcomes in infancy (≤2 months) or early childhood (3–7 years), compared with standard care/no lifestyle intervention.

included_articles.title: Lifestyle intervention in obese pregnancy and cardiac remodelling in 3-year olds: children of the UPBEAT RCT.
Cardiac function in newborns of obese women and the effect of exercise during pregnancy. A randomized controlled trial.
Adiposity and cardiovascular outcomes in three-year-old children of participants in UPBEAT, an RCT of a complex intervention in pregnant women with obesity.
Preconception lifestyle intervention in women with obesity and echocardiographic indices of cardiovascular health in their children.
A preconception lifestyle intervention in women with obesity and cardiovascular health in their children.
The Effects of a Preconception Lifestyle Intervention on Childhood Cardiometabolic Health-Follow-Up of a Randomized Controlled Trial.
